input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm naming
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows SDK dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires the Windows SDK and build tools:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add the needed workload components via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --wait
```

> **Or**, if using full Visual Studio:
> ```powershell
> winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
> ```
> Then select the **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload in the installer UI.

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a default Android SDK, but you may need to manage versions:

```powershell
# Discover where the Android SDK was installed
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Or find it at the default location:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
$sdkManager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# If cmdline-tools aren't present, install them:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# List available platforms & build-tools dynamically
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"

# Install the latest stable platform + build tools (adjust versions from above)
& $sdkManager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkManager --licenses
```

## 5. Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& $sdkManager "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Ensure Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled for emulator acceleration
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All |
  Select-Object State

# Enable if needed (requires reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI/MAUI Windows apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# See which TFMs a MAUI template supports
dotnet new maui -n TfmCheck --dry-run
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' TfmCheck/TfmCheck.csproj

# List installed Android platform versions
& $sdkManager --list_installed | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# List installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Select-Object Name
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net10.0` if you install .NET 10 instead (available as preview).
- Android API level (`android-35`) and build-tools versions should match what `--list` reports as latest stable.
- Running the Windows target requires the machine to be Windows 10 19041+ or Windows 11.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide from my knowledge.
